title: Pre-review verdict
description: Codex output for a single PreToolUse review submission (file_change only — v2.1 removed the bash_command path). Strict-mode compatible: additionalProperties:false on every object, every property in required.

decision.description: Maps directly to PreToolUse permissionDecision. `allow`: the change is safe to apply; `deny`: the change has a clear problem; `ask`: needs operator approval.

reason.description: Short, human-readable explanation. Surfaced to Claude as permissionDecisionReason.

findings.description: Per-issue detail when decision != allow. Empty array is valid (use it when the reason is self-contained).

contradicts_grounding.description: v2.1 rail (spec §5.1): set true when this finding's category is one a deterministic tool covers (gofmt/golangci-lint for style, staticcheck/go vet for unused/deadcode, gosec for taint, govulncheck for vulns, race for visible races) AND that tool passed clean on the cited line AND you have no reproducible failure to cite. NEVER set true on categories the tools cannot judge: safety, correctness, data_loss, blast_radius, design. When true, the engine demotes the finding to display-only so it cannot block.
